objects.name: idx_drive_account
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_drive_account      ON drive_links(account)

objects.name: idx_drive_rfc822
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_drive_rfc822       ON drive_links(rfc822_id)

objects.name: idx_emails_account
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_account     ON emails(account)

objects.name: idx_emails_category
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_category    ON emails(category)

objects.name: idx_emails_date_sent
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_date_sent   ON emails(date_sent)

objects.name: idx_emails_from
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_from        ON emails(from_addr)

objects.name: idx_emails_has_body
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_has_body    ON emails(has_body)

objects.name: idx_emails_refs
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_refs ON emails(refs)

objects.name: idx_emails_reply_to
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_reply_to ON emails(in_reply_to)

objects.name: idx_emails_rfc822
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_rfc822      ON emails(rfc822_id)

objects.name: idx_emails_spam
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_spam        ON emails(is_spam)

objects.name: idx_emails_subject
objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_subject ON emails(subject)

objects.name: chain_of_custody
objects.sql:
CREATE TABLE chain_of_custody (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    source_path     TEXT,
    source_type     TEXT,
    sha256          TEXT,
    size_bytes      INTEGER,
    case_id         TEXT,
    notes           TEXT,
    ingested_at     TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
)

objects.name: drive_links
objects.sql:
CREATE TABLE drive_links (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    rfc822_id       TEXT NOT NULL,
    account         TEXT NOT NULL,
    gmail_id        TEXT,
    drive_url       TEXT NOT NULL,
    drive_item_id   TEXT
)

objects.name: emails
objects.sql:
CREATE TABLE emails (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    rfc822_id       TEXT NOT NULL,
    gmail_id        TEXT,
    filename        TEXT,                   -- FileName from metadata CSV
    account         TEXT NOT NULL,
    labels_raw      TEXT,                   -- Raw comma-separated Gmail labels
    category        TEXT,                   -- Derived: inbox/sent/draft/spam/trash/deleted/archived
    from_addr       TEXT,
    subject         TEXT,
    to_addr         TEXT,
    cc_addr         TEXT,
    bcc_addr        TEXT,
    date_sent       TEXT,
    date_received   TEXT,
    thread_count    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    locker_source   TEXT NOT NULL,          -- ALL / LEGAL / SG
    zip_source      TEXT,                   -- Which zip file contained the mbox
    mbox_source     TEXT,                   -- Which .mbox file inside the zip
    body            TEXT,                   -- Full body (Phase 2)
    body_single     TEXT,                   -- First-message-only body (Phase 2)
    has_body        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    is_spam         INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    is_draft        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    is_trash        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')), in_reply_to TEXT, refs TEXT,
    UNIQUE(rfc822_id, account)
)

objects.name: emails_fts
objects.sql:
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE emails_fts USING fts5(
    subject, body, from_addr, to_addr,
    content='emails',
    content_rowid='id'
)

objects.name: emails_fts_config
objects.sql: CREATE TABLE 'emails_fts_config'(k PRIMARY KEY, v) WITHOUT ROWID

objects.name: emails_fts_data
objects.sql: CREATE TABLE 'emails_fts_data'(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, block BLOB)

objects.name: emails_fts_docsize
objects.sql: CREATE TABLE 'emails_fts_docsize'(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, sz BLOB)

objects.name: emails_fts_idx
objects.sql: CREATE TABLE 'emails_fts_idx'(segid, term, pgno, PRIMARY KEY(segid, term)) WITHOUT ROWID

objects.name: sqlite_sequence
objects.sql: CREATE TABLE sqlite_sequence(name,seq)

objects.name: emails_fts_ad
objects.sql:
CREATE TRIGGER emails_fts_ad AFTER DELETE ON emails BEGIN
    INSERT INTO emails_fts(emails_fts, rowid, subject, body, from_addr, to_addr)
    VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.subject, old.body, old.from_addr, old.to_addr);
END

objects.name: emails_fts_ai
objects.sql:
CREATE TRIGGER emails_fts_ai AFTER INSERT ON emails BEGIN
    INSERT INTO emails_fts(rowid, subject, body, from_addr, to_addr)
    VALUES (new.id, new.subject, new.body, new.from_addr, new.to_addr);
END

objects.name: emails_fts_au

emails.body:
Tips for using your new inbox
[image: Gmail]

Welcome to your inbox
[image: Search]
Find emails fast
With the power of Google Search in your inbox, you can archive
<https://support.google.com/mail/answer/6576?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>
all your email and find it
<https://support.google.com/mail/answer/6593?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>
later in a flash. Your inbox stays tidy, and you never need to worry about
what to keep.
[image: Personalize]
Personalize rowboat creative Mail
Customize inbox backgrounds, email signatures, and other options in rowboat
creative Mail settings
<https://support.google.com/mail/answer/6562?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>.
Review and adjust your privacy and security settings at My Account
<https://myaccount.google.com/>.
[image: Organize]
Organize your email
You can organize email with labels
<https://support.google.com/mail/answer/118708?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>,
which are like folders, but you can add more than one label to a message.
You can also use filters
<https://support.google.com/mail/answer/6579?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>
to automatically sort and categorize messages as they come in.
[image: Social]
Chat and meet from your inbox
Chat with contacts and start video meetings with up to 25 people in Google
Hangouts
<https://support.google.com/hangouts/answer/3115176?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>
.

Get training and tips
Find Gmail guides, tips, cheat sheets, help switching from Microsoft®
Outlook®, and more at the Google Workspace Learning Center
<https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/9259748?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>
.
Visit the Learning Center
<https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/9259748?hl=en&utm_source=gmailwelcomeemail&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gmailwelcome>

Happy emailing,
The Gmail Team
[image: Google Cloud]

© 2022 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

*You’re receiving this mandatory email service announcement to update you
about important changes to your Google Cloud product or account.*

      $0.99/pcs Folding Chair


    
                
              

            
              
                
                  
      Hi Morning
I want to share with you a very useful product.
This folding chair has an ergonomic handle that folds and is easy to carry. Simply lift the handle, and you can fold it easily with one hand in an instant. Portable chair quickly and easily folds for simple storage and transportation.
Designed with many excellent characteristics, such as high stability, strong load-bearing and sturdy, our folding chair can with stand the weight of an adult, and no need to worry about deformation. Perfect for all outdoor adventures.
It is constructed of plastic material. And there are 2 models to choose from. This product is perfectly suitable for camping, hiking,travelling, fishing, and more


    
                
              

            
              
                
                  
      
        
          
            
              
        
          
      
    
        
      
            
          

        
      
    
                
              

            
              
                
                  
      Unit Price:
US$0.99/PCS  Small Size :23*19*18CM
US$1.49/PCS  Big Size :25*31*22.3CM
Material:  Plastic 
Packing: Polybag
Quotation based on EXWork
Logo: Free for 1color
Production time: 18-21work days
Above prices base on 10000pcs

objects.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_message_id ON emails(message_id)

objects.name: emails
objects.sql:
CREATE TABLE emails (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            zip_file TEXT,
            mbox_name TEXT,
            email_account TEXT,
            message_id TEXT,
            date TEXT,
            from_addr TEXT,
            to_addr TEXT,
            cc_addr TEXT,
            subject TEXT,
            body_snippet TEXT,
            thread_id TEXT
        , labels TEXT)

objects.name: emails_fts
objects.sql:
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE emails_fts USING fts5(
            subject,
            body_snippet,
            content='emails',
            content_rowid='id'
        )

objects.name: emails_fts_config
objects.sql: CREATE TABLE 'emails_fts_config'(k PRIMARY KEY, v) WITHOUT ROWID

objects.sql: CREATE TABLE 'emails_fts_data'(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, block BLOB)

objects.name: emails_ai
objects.sql:
CREATE TRIGGER emails_ai AFTER INSERT ON emails BEGIN
            INSERT INTO emails_fts(rowid, subject, body_snippet)
            VALUES (new.id, new.subject, new.body_snippet);
        END

emails.body_snippet:
Hey Jay -

Everything good on your end?? We've been worried!

Bunch of outstanding items:

*FOUR - *
-Need pricing on UV shirts
-UV design -- want big gold flag on back (reflective is possible or screen
print metallic) - so the whole shirt changes color but the gold outline
flat stays gold, but the inside of the stripes and stars should still
UV -- *does
this work / make sense?*
-Need input on the jackets (in your possession)
-Need to redo the jackets with the 3m gold reflective (or comparable
material)

As mentioned, there are some very high profile Chicagoans who are
interested in gear, and would love to get jackets (at a minimum) in
their possession so that we can strike while the iron is hot.

*Hunt Club - *
Need a quote on a ton of hats & shirts (dad hats) -- have a new logo we are
launching soon and want to get in front of this.

I can send you a higher res logo but here is the attached - teal'ish key
with white HUNT CLUB font next to it.  Will want to put this one a navy
shirt, and across a dad hat.

*2 questions -- *

*1) will this fit across a dad hat? Is the key too intricate to embroider
(to note, the teeth of the key is like a computer chip so its got some
detail) -- otherwise we may just go Key like last time on front, and Hunt
Club across the back*

*2) what colors of navy shirts could look good? We did bella canvas navy --
the soft ones -- open to suggestions.*

*3) you guys dont do other products right..like water bottles etc?*

*On all of the above would love to save cost where we can so will defer to
you on whats best. *

Much appreciated,
Sami
emails.subject: Checking in /// FOUR /// Hunt Club -- **Time Sensitive**

emails.to_addr:
Lucas Guariglia <lucas@rowboatcreative.com>,
        Jay Goebel
	<jay@rowboatcreative.com>,
        Joe Zangrilli <joe@rowboatcreative.com>

emails.body_snippet:
hi Lucas,

thanks for the rundown below. a few questions based off these possible
changes:


   - how would this change in machinery change the quantity output?
   - if you could still break this down for me a bit based off each line
   item that would be great

also, i received a few renderings of what we are looking to build out. take
a look and let me know what you need in order to price out. there might be
a few updates to the renderings when i get the final ones on monday and i
will send through for viz. If possible, can i get top line costs by
thursday of next week? For pricing, are you able to list out individual
costs for each piece?

thanks!
destiny greco-ramirez
1640 wilcox ave
los angeles, ca 90028
<https://maps.google.com/?q=1640+Wilcox+Ave,+Los+Angeles,+CA+90028,+USA>
c. 661 877 7785 o. 323 843 4294
www.narrativemediagroup.com
instagram: @narrativegroup <https://www.instagram.com/narrativegroup/>

new york | los angeles | chicago

This email and any files transmitted are confidential and intended solely
for the use of the individual or entity to which they are addressed.If you
have received this email by error, please notify us immediately and
permanently delete it from your computer.


On Thu, Aug 29, 2019 at 6:43 AM Lucas Guariglia <lucas@rowboatcreative.com>
wrote:

> Morning Destiny!
> Happy to work on scope to keep closer to intended budget. One immediate
> solve might be to reduce to 1qty screen printing press and then couple that
> with the 2 heat press stations where consumers can pick from pre-printed
> transfers of different designs and color ways to layer onto the tee prints.
> This would still make a strong visual impact with the actual printing press
> and heat presses, however we could cut back on staffing as well as
> machinery. This would also consolidate the footprint if that is also
> concern.
>
> Let me know initial thoughts on the above and we can keep working together
> to align on ide
emails.subject: Re: Onsite Screen printing 10/17

emails_fts.body_snippet:
Hey Jay -

Everything good on your end?? We've been worried!

Bunch of outstanding items:

*FOUR - *
-Need pricing on UV shirts
-UV design -- want big gold flag on back (reflective is possible or screen
print metallic) - so the whole shirt changes color but the gold outline
flat stays gold, but the inside of the stripes and stars should still
UV -- *does
this work / make sense?*
-Need input on the jackets (in your possession)
-Need to redo the jackets with the 3m gold reflective (or comparable
material)

As mentioned, there are some very high profile Chicagoans who are
interested in gear, and would love to get jackets (at a minimum) in
their possession so that we can strike while the iron is hot.

*Hunt Club - *
Need a quote on a ton of hats & shirts (dad hats) -- have a new logo we are
launching soon and want to get in front of this.

I can send you a higher res logo but here is the attached - teal'ish key
with white HUNT CLUB font next to it.  Will want to put this one a navy
shirt, and across a dad hat.

*2 questions -- *

*1) will this fit across a dad hat? Is the key too intricate to embroider
(to note, the teeth of the key is like a computer chip so its got some
detail) -- otherwise we may just go Key like last time on front, and Hunt
Club across the back*

*2) what colors of navy shirts could look good? We did bella canvas navy --
the soft ones -- open to suggestions.*

*3) you guys dont do other products right..like water bottles etc?*

*On all of the above would love to save cost where we can so will defer to
you on whats best. *

Much appreciated,
Sami
emails_fts.subject: Checking in /// FOUR /// Hunt Club -- **Time Sensitive**

objects.name: chain_of_custody
objects.sql:
CREATE TABLE chain_of_custody (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
            source_type TEXT NOT NULL,
            sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
            size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,
            case_id TEXT,
            notes TEXT,
            ingested_at TEXT NOT NULL
        )

objects.name: sqlite_sequence
objects.sql: CREATE TABLE sqlite_sequence(name,seq)

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM emails WHERE rfc822_id IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(rfc822_id) <> '';
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT rfc822_id) FROM emails WHERE rfc822_id IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(rfc822_id) <> '';
SELECT locker_source, COUNT(*) FROM emails GROUP BY locker_source ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, locker_source;
SELECT zip_source, COUNT(*) FROM emails GROUP BY zip_source ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, zip_source;
SELECT mbox_source, COUNT(*) FROM emails GROUP BY mbox_source ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, mbox_source;
SELECT account, COUNT(*) FROM emails GROUP BY account ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, account;
SELECT gmail_id, COUNT(*) c FROM emails WHERE gmail_id IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(gmail_id) <> '' GROUP BY gmail_id HAVING c > 1 ORDER BY c DESC, gmail_id LIMIT 20;
SELECT rfc822_id, COUNT(*) c FROM emails WHERE rfc822_id IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(rfc822_id) <> '' GROUP BY rfc822_id HAVING c > 1 ORDER BY c DESC, rfc822_id LIMIT 20;
